quick_facts.label: Occupational reference
quick_facts.value: The National Career Service (Ministry of Labour & Employment) lists “Marketing Manager – Line of Business” under NCO 2015 code 3322.1701; its profile is automotive-sector specific, so it is useful as an Indian reference point rather than a universal requirement for every marketing-manager vacancy.

quick_facts.label: Core purpose
quick_facts.value: Marketing managers translate business objectives into marketing strategy, use customer and competitor evidence, shape branding and product-portfolio activity, and oversee delivery of marketing plans.

quick_facts.label: Education context
quick_facts.value: UGC’s specified-degree material identifies BBA and BMS as three-year bachelor’s programmes entered after 10+2, and MBA as a two-year master’s programme entered after a bachelor’s degree. These are education pathways, not a statutory licence requirement to hold a marketing-manager job.

quick_facts.label: Indicative progression
quick_facts.value: NCS’s automotive profile shows Marketing Manager – Line of Business progressing to General Manager, while expressly noting that progression is indicative; actual titles and promotion criteria vary by employer and sector.

sections.heading: What a Marketing Manager does
paragraphs: A Marketing Manager owns the planning-to-execution cycle for marketing activity: developing strategy against company objectives; assessing customer research, market conditions, and competitor information; designing branding and marketing activity for a product portfolio; supervising plan implementation; and managing the product life cycle from strategic through tactical planning. In practice, the balance between online channels, offline activation, product marketing, brand management, and sales enablement depends on the employer’s business model.

The NCS reference is specifically for an automotive “Line of Business” role. It nevertheless provides a useful evidence-based description of the managerial work that commonly underpins the broader title, rather than a single mandatory job specification for all Indian employers.
bullets: Set measurable marketing objectives and convert them into plans, campaigns, and execution priorities.
Use market, customer, and competitor evidence to inform positioning, portfolio, and communication choices.
Coordinate implementation and manage marketing personnel or assistants where the role has people-management responsibility.
Track commercial inputs such as budgets, expenditure, return-on-investment considerations, and profit-and-loss projections where these fall within the role’s remit.

sections.heading: Entry routes in India
paragraphs: A practical route is to complete 10+2, pursue a bachelor’s programme that develops business, commerce, management, communication, analytics, or marketing capability, gain relevant experience in digital marketing, brand, sales, research, content, or campaign roles, and progress into marketing management as scope and leadership responsibility grow. UGC lists BBA and BMS among specified three-year bachelor’s degrees with 10+2 entry, and MBA as a two-year master’s degree with a bachelor’s entry qualification.

Marketing-focused undergraduate study can provide directly relevant foundations. A UGC curriculum for B.A. (Vocational Studies) in Marketing Management and Retail Business includes marketing management, marketing research, consumer behaviour, segmentation and positioning, advertising and sales promotion, business communication, an internship/project, financial management, and e-commerce. Programme availability, admissions criteria, curriculum, and employer preferences must be checked with the particular institution or employer.

An MBA or other postgraduate management study can be an optional route, particularly for candidates seeking structured management education; it is not established by the cited sources as a universal legal or occupational prerequisite for this role.
bullets: Build demonstrable campaign, research, content, CRM, paid-media, brand, sales, or product-marketing work through internships and early-career roles.
Use portfolios and quantified outcomes—such as leads, conversion, reach, retention, revenue contribution, or research insights—to evidence readiness for broader responsibility.
Verify recognition, admission rules, programme delivery mode, fees, and placement claims directly with the institution before enrolling.

sections.heading: Skills to develop
paragraphs: The role combines commercial judgement with research, communication, execution, and leadership. NCS identifies market research, competitor comparison, identifying potential customers, communication strategy, market-trend analysis, customer-feedback methods, budget and expenditure awareness, return-on-investment and profit-and-loss concepts, distribution strategy, MS Office, and organisation-specific MIS knowledge as relevant competencies for its automotive marketing-manager profile.

For candidates moving up from digital marketing, digital channel expertise is most useful when paired with the wider management capabilities needed to connect audience insight, brand positioning, budget decisions, campaign operations, and team or agency coordination.
bullets: Market and customer research; competitor and trend analysis.
Segmentation, target-market selection, positioning, and brand communication.
Campaign planning and implementation across appropriate channels.
Budget awareness, performance measurement, and return-on-investment reasoning.
Clear written and verbal communication, stakeholder coordination, and people leadership.
Working fluency with productivity tools and the organisation’s reporting or MIS systems.

sections.heading: Work setting and conditions
paragraphs: Work settings differ across consumer businesses, services, technology companies, agencies, manufacturers, and business-to-business firms. The NCS automotive profile describes a role that may require managing marketing staff or assistants and local travel, and says overtime may be required. It also reports five or six working days and eight or nine hours per day as a usual pattern for that sectoral profile, while stating that arrangements vary by organisation.

Candidates should treat any advertised location, hybrid arrangement, travel expectation, working hours, reporting line, team size, and budget authority as employer-specific terms to confirm during recruitment.
bullets: Often cross-functional: marketing managers coordinate with sales, product, finance, agencies, vendors, and leadership.
May combine desk-based analysis and planning with market visits, activations, events, partner coordination, or customer-facing work.
May supervise internal staff and/or external agencies, depending on company size and marketing model.

sections.heading: Career progression
paragraphs: A common progression pattern is from specialist or executive roles—such as digital marketing, content, performance marketing, market research, brand, sales marketing, or product marketing—toward Marketing Manager as the candidate takes ownership of plans, budgets, cross-functional delivery, and teams. From there, progression can lead to senior marketing, brand, category, product-marketing, or general-management roles, but titles are not standardised across Indian employers.

NCS’s sector-specific automotive pathway maps Marketing Manager – Line of Business to General Manager and labels the path indicative. That supports treating progression as a direction of increasing commercial and leadership scope, not as a guaranteed or universal title sequence.
bullets: Early stage: execute campaigns, analyse results, support research, and build channel or functional expertise.
Manager stage: own plans, coordinate stakeholders, allocate resources, supervise execution, and report business outcomes.
Senior stage: lead larger portfolios, brands, categories, regions, or marketing functions; progression may move toward general management.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: There is no single nationally fixed salary for Marketing Managers. Pay varies materially with city, sector, employer size, portfolio scale, prior experience, variable-pay design, and the degree of people or budget responsibility. As a dated and sector-specific reference only, NCS’s automotive Marketing Manager – Line of Business profile, last updated 18 September 2023, listed indicative earnings for experienced candidates of ₹25,000–₹41,000 per month and explicitly stated that the figures were subject to change.

Use that historical NCS range only as context—not as a current market benchmark or offer expectation. Compare current written offers on total fixed pay, variable pay, benefits, role scope, work location, travel requirements, notice terms, and any applicable statutory wage protections. The Code on Wages, 2019 provides for minimum rates of wages and a Central Government floor wage framework, but it does not prescribe one Marketing Manager salary nationwide.
bullets: Ask whether stated CTC includes variable incentive, employer provident-fund contributions, insurance, gratuity accrual, reimbursements, or joining/retention payments.
Compare pay with the actual role scope: individual-contributor versus team lead, local versus national remit, and campaign execution versus portfolio ownership.
Treat salary-survey figures and job-board estimates as time-bound market signals; verify the terms in the employer’s written offer.
